author: p-durandin
title: CachingTest LoadMultiWithConfig_race import_model call count mismatch on Windows
signature: Actual function call count doesnt match EXPECT_CALL import_model Expected called times Actual called times unsatisfied and active|Flaky Test

failed_jobs.name: C++ unit tests / C++ unit tests

key_errors:
caching_test.cpp(2667): error: Actual function call count doesn't match EXPECT_CALL(*mockPlugin, import_model(A<std::istream&>(), _))...
         Expected: to be called 8 times
           Actual: called 7 times - unsatisfied and active
root_cause: The test LoadMultiWithConfig_race stresses concurrent model loading with caching enabled, expecting a fixed number of import_model calls. Under Windows CI runner load variability, a race in scheduling threads causes one import_model call to be elided or deduplicated by the caching layer, resulting in the mock expectation of 8 calls being satisfied only 7 times. This is a known intermittent timing-sensitive test that has failed before (prior occurrence: 2026-07-13, run 29234771303, PR #36488). The merged PR #36872 only touches GPU plugin linking order and is unrelated to this test or caching code.
reproduction_steps: Run the test on a Windows machine with high load: ov_inference_functional_tests --gtest_filter=CachingTest/CachingTest.LoadMultiWithConfig_race/ByModelName_testCache --gtest_repeat=20
recommended_actions: Re-run the failed jobs — this is a flaky race-condition test unrelated to the merged PR
Consider marking LoadMultiWithConfig_race as DISABLED_* on Windows or adding retry logic in the test
Track occurrences of this flaky test to determine if it needs a permanent fix to synchronization logic in the caching code
prevention_strategies: Add retry logic or tolerance for timing-sensitive caching tests on Windows, or move race tests to a separate suite that allows N retries before marking as failed.
historical_context: Same signature seen on 2026-07-13 (run 29234771303, PR #36488). Pattern count is now 2.
